item.title: Indonesia sends letter of reprimand to YouTube over breach of social media curbs, minister says
item.description: Indonesia has sent Google a letter of reprimand because its YouTube platform has not вҖӢcomplied with new social media curbs for children, вҖҢa senior minister said, the first such sanction since the rules took effect last month.

item.title: Meta pulls ads aimed at recruiting plaintiffs for social media addiction lawsuits
item.description: Meta Platforms said on Thursday it is pulling ads from Facebook and Instagram aimed at recruiting new plaintiffs вҖӢfor ongoing litigation accusing it and other social media companies of designing their platforms to be addictive to young users.

item.title: Exclusive: Anthropic weighs building it own AI chips, sources say
item.description: Artificial intelligence lab Anthropic is exploring the possibility of designing its own chips, вҖӢthree sources said, as the company and its rivals respond to вҖҢa shortage of AI chips needed to power and develop more advanced AI systems.

item.title: Fitch cuts FS KKR Capital rating to junk territory as asset quality weakens
item.description: Fitch Ratings on Thursday downgraded its rating on a private credit fund jointly run by Future Standard and KKR вҖӢto junk territory, citing continued deterioration in its asset quality.

item.title: Elon Musk's xAI sues Colorado over state's new AI law
item.description: xAI filed a lawsuit on Thursday seeking to block Colorado from enforcing a new law regulating artificial intelligence systems, escalating a fight over whether вҖӢoversight should be handled by states or by Washington.

item.title: Meta transfers top engineers into new AI tooling team
item.description: Meta is drafting top software engineers from across the company into a new AI engineering organization created last month, according to an internal memo seen by вҖӢReuters, part of a reorganization of its workforce in preparation for layoffs.

item.title: Ecuador raises tariffs on Colombia to 100% from 50%
item.description: Ecuador's government on Thursday said it was raising tariffs вҖӢon imports from its larger neighbor Colombia to вҖҢ100% from a prior level of 50%, citing Colombia's alleged failure to implement border security measures.

item.title: White House warned staff not to place market bets amid Iran war, WSJ reports
item.description: The White House warned U.S. вҖӢgovernment staff against improperly вҖҢleveraging their positions to place bets in вҖӢfutures markets вҖӢin an email on March вҒ 23, the вҖӢWall Street Journal reported вҖӢon Thursday, citing sources.

item.title: Trump says Iran should not charge fees to tankers going through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Donald Trump said on Thursday Iran should not вҖӢcharge fees to tankers going through the Strait of Hormuz, which has вҖҢbeen blockaded since the start of the Iran war, causing the worst disruption to global energy supplies in history.

item.title: CarMax settles with activist Starboard with two new board appointments
item.description: CarMax said on Thursday it would add Bill Cobb and Jim Kessler to its вҖӢboard in connection with its вҖҢ2026 annual shareholder meeting, after activist investor Starboard Value pushed for changes вҖӢat the used-car retailer.

item.title: TRADING DAY Ceasefire sends stocks higher
item.description: U.S. stocks rallied on Thursday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq clocking their seventh daily gain, as investors shrugged off a rise in oil prices and pinned their hopes on the fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire extending to Israel and Lebanon too.

item.title: US considers new crackdown on Chinese telecom companies, FCC says
item.description: The U.S. Federal Communications вҖӢCommission said it may вҖҢbar three major Chinese telecom companies вҖӢfrom operating data вҖӢcenters in the United вҒ States and ban вҖӢother carriers from вҖӢconnecting with them in the United States.

item.title: USTR's Greer sees no changes in data rules that bar Chinese vehicles from US
item.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said Thursday that President Donald Trump's administration does not plan any changes вҖҢto a crackdown on vehicle hardware and software from China, which effectively bars Chinese cars from the U.S. market.

item.title: StubHub to pay $10 million to settle FTC ticket price case
item.description: StubHub Holdings on Thursday agreed to pay $10 million to settle U.S. Federal Trade Commission вҖӢcharges that the platform failed to disclose вҖҢtotal ticket price for live events to consumers.

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike stamp prices to 82 cents
item.description: The cash-strapped U.S. Postal Service said on Thursday it вҖӢwants to raise the price of first-class mail stamps to вҖҢ82 cents from 78 cents effective July 12.

item.title: US Treasury yield forecasts creep up, but strategists cling to benign inflation view
item.description: U.S. Treasury yield forecasts are only a bit higher than a month ago despite the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, according to market strategists polled by Reuters who are still putting off making major changes to their longer-term inflation view.

item.title: CFO Anthony Armstrong leaves xAI, the Information reports
item.description: Anthony Armstrong, named xAI's CFO in October, has departed the company as part of a вҖӢbroader wave of senior exits, the Information вҖҢreported on Thursday, citing two people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US spirits maker Sazerac explores deal with Jack Daniel's owner, source says
item.description: Privately held U.S. drinks maker Sazerac вҖӢis exploring a deal for BrownвҖ‘Forman, according to a source familiar with вҖҢthe matter, potentially emerging as a rival suitor for the Jack DanielвҖҷs maker that is already in talks with FranceвҖҷs Pernod Ricard over a possible merger.

item.title: Iran war raises demand for US fuel, boosting Gulf Coast refining margins
item.description: U.S. Gulf Coast refiners are reaping the strongest margins in years as disruptions to Middle Eastern oil flows from the Iran war raise demand for U.S. fuel exports, вҖӢanalysts and experts said.

item.title: Berkshire unit must face brokerage commission lawsuit despite $250 million settlement, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge вҖӢruled on Thursday that a Berkshire Hathaway unit must face a вҖҢproposed antitrust class action claiming it conspired to inflate commissions that home sellers pay real estate agents, even though its HomeServices of America brokerage settled the same claims for $250 вҖӢmillion in 2024.

item.title: Paramount seals debt commitments backing Warner Bros deal
item.description: Paramount Skydance has syndicated its bridge loan facility and secured permanent financing from a group of вҖӢbanks for its planned $111 billion acquisition of Warner Bros вҖҢDiscovery .

item.title: Tesla's lower-cost EV plan seen boosting volume, risking margins
item.description: Tesla investors said вҖӢthe company's plan to develop a smaller, lower-cost EV, which was reported exclusively by Reuters, might boost вҖҢvolumes and address slowing demand, but at the cost of already strained margins.

item.title: McKinsey to contribute $125 million to Purdue bankruptcy over opioid sales advice
item.description: Consulting firm McKinsey & Co has agreed to contribute $125 million to Purdue Pharma's bankruptcy settlement, resolving potential legal claims over advice it provided to Purdue on вҖӢhow to вҖңturbochargeвҖқ sales of its addictive painkiller OxyContin, according to a court вҖҢfiling submitted late Wednesday.

item.title: American Airlines set to resume US flights to Venezuela as soon as April 30
item.description: American Airlines said вҖӢThursday it aims вҖҢto resume U.S. flights вҖӢto Venezuela вҖӢas soon as April вҒ 30 after вҖӢwinning approval from вҖӢthe Transportation Department last month.

item.title: US spirits maker Sazerac explores deal with Brown-Forman, WSJ reports
item.description: Alcoholic beverage вҖӢmaker Sazerac is exploring вҖҢa deal with Jack Daniel's maker Brown-Forman , the вҖӢWall Street Journal вҖӢreported on Thursday, citing people вҒ familiar with the вҖӢmatter.

item.title: Airbus delivers 60 aircrafts in March
item.description: Airbus delivered 60 aircrafts in March, bringing first-quarter deliveries to вҖӢ114 jets, down 16% from вҖҢthe same point last year, the European planemaker said on Thursday.

item.title: VW to stop making its top electric vehicle in the US
item.description: Volkswagen said on Thursday it will end production of вҖӢthe ID.4 electric SUV out of вҖҢits Tennessee plant this month, citing a challenging time for the U.S. electric vehicle вҖӢmarket.

item.title: US software stocks fall as Anthropic's new AI model revives disruption fears
item.description: U.S. software shares tumbled on Thursday after Anthropic held back the wide release of a powerful AI model over concerns вҖӢit could expose hidden cybersecurity vulnerabilities, deepening investor fears about вҖҢthe threat to traditional software firms.

item.title: Florida AG to probe OpenAI and ChatGPT
item.description: Florida вҖӢAttorney вҖҢGeneral вҖӢJames вҖӢUthmeier вҒ on вҖӢThursday вҖӢlaunched вҖӢan вҖӢinvestigation into вҖҢOpenAI вҒ and вҖӢits вҖӢpopular вҒ chatbot вҖӢChatGPT.

item.title: Brazil court removes BYD from list of firms linked to forced labor
item.description: A Brazilian court вҖӢissued вҖӢan injunction вҒ removing вҖӢChinese вҖӢautomaker BYD from a вҖӢgovernment вҖӢlist of вҖҢfirms вҒ accused of employing workers вҖӢin slavery-like вҖӢconditions, вҒ a вҖӢdocument вҖӢseen вҒ by Reuters вҒ on вҖӢThursday вҖӢshowed.
